resources.title: Abraham Lincoln: An Initial Biography
resources.author: Genevieve Foster
resources.rationale: This classic biography is back in print! Foster presents the life of Abraham Lincoln through an engaging narrative that starts with his humble beginnings and takes the reader through his political rise, his presidency during the Civil War, and his assassination and legacy.
resources.note: Please use the Option 1 link to utilize the discount code.

resources.title: Adoniram Judson: Danger on the Streets of Gold
resources.author: Irene Howat
resources.rationale: The first missionary ever sent from America, Adoniram Judson (1788-1850), faced many challenges as he ministered in the country of Burma. Although he faced many personal trials and even imprisonment by the government, he rejoiced that his translation work and ministry to individuals reached people with the gospel of Jesus Christ.
resources.note: Please use Option 1 to utilize the discount code.

resources.title: An Honorable Heritage: The Pandita Ramabai Story in Her Own Words
resources.author: Pandita Ramabai
resources.rationale: Pandita Ramabai (1858-1922) was a social reformer, an advocate for the emancipation of women, and a pioneer in education. She tells her testimony of being born into a privileged Brahman caste in India and leaving it all behind to become a Christian. It is a fascinating read!
resources.note: Use option one link to receive discount.

resources.title: Getty-Dubay® Italic Handwriting Book B
resources.author: Handwriting Success
resources.maySub: penmanship model of your choice or Penny Gardner
resources.rationale: For the beginning reader, this book presents lowercase, capitals and numerals. With a short word and lively sentence on each page, this book engages the student.
resources.note: Choose this OR Italics by Penny Gardner. You do not need the Instruction Manual. Necessary instructions will be included in the lesson plans.

resources.title: Getty-Dubay® Italic Handwriting Book C
resources.author: Handwriting Success
resources.maySub: penmanship model of your choice or Penny Gardner
resources.rationale: The simple transition to cursive italic is introduced. Basic writing practice themes include: modes of transportation (automobile through zeppelin), days of the week, months of the year, and sentences. Capitals practice: cities of the USA. Includes numerals.
resources.note: Choose this OR Italics by Penny Gardner. You do not need the Instruction Manual. Necessary instructions will be included in the lesson plans.

resources.title: Getty-Dubay® Italic Handwriting Book D
resources.author: Handwriting Success
resources.maySub: penmanship model of your choice or Penny Gardner
resources.rationale: Writing practice for basic italic and cursive italic includes capitalization, vowel and consonant sounds, prefixes, suffixes, phonograms, homophones, tongue twisters, and six poem forms. Cursive capitals are introduced with historical development of each letter.
resources.note: Choose this OR Italics by Penny Gardner. You do not need the Instruction Manual. Necessary instructions will be included in the lesson plans.

resources.title: Getty-Dubay® Italic Handwriting Book E
resources.author: Handwriting Success
resources.maySub: penmanship model of your choice or Penny Gardner
resources.rationale: While practicing cursive italic, students explore the many facets of the world of science — the animal kingdom, DNA, photosynthesis, minerals, continents, biomes, our solar system, and galaxy. Practice with capitals: cities of the world and history of letters.
resources.note: Choose this OR Italics by Penny Gardner. You do not need the Instruction Manual. Necessary instructions will be included in the lesson plans.

resources.title: Getty-Dubay® Italic Handwriting Book F
resources.author: Handwriting Success
resources.maySub: penmanship model of your choice or Penny Gardner
resources.rationale: Figures of speech in the English language are used for cursive writing practice. Students will practice capital letters using the names of First Nation tribes and communities of North America. The exercises also explore the history of letters and numerals.
resources.note: Choose this OR Italics by Penny Gardner. You do not need the Instruction Manual. Necessary instructions will be included in the lesson plans.

resources.title: Getty-Dubay® Italic Handwriting Book G
resources.author: Handwriting Success
resources.maySub: penmanship model of your choice or Penny Gardner
resources.rationale: The final book in the Series contains basic and cursive writing practice to acquaint the student with the history of writing.  Part 3: edged pen writing.
resources.note: Choose this OR Italics by Penny Gardner. You do not need the Instruction Manual. Necessary instructions will be included in the lesson plans.

resources.title: God's Bible Timeline: The Big Book of Biblical History
resources.author: Linda Finlayson
resources.rationale: Contains age-appropriate commentary on the eras of the Bible, along with detailed timelines, known dates, and a timeline poster. Lessons will refer to the timeline when reviewing stories and events.
resources.note: Use option one link to receive discount.

resources.title: Mapping the World with Art
resources.author: Ellen Johnston McHenry
resources.rationale: This book is a blend of history, geography, and art. The text is about the history of cartography, from the earliest maps that were drawn on clay by the people of Mesopotamia to the exploration of the "new world."  This book also includes map drawing lessons that take students step by step through the process of drawing maps of all the countries mentioned in the text.
resources.note: We highly recommend purchasing the videos that guide students through the map drawings. The option linked includes a digital version of the book along with the video course, but you can also purchase the videos in DVD or USB format if you prefer a physical copy of the book. The discount code is valid for all products on the author's site.

resources.title: Master of Field Arts
resources.author: Roseann Hanson
resources.rationale: Welcome to the next level for nature journalers and field sketchers—a deep dive into becoming a dedicated master naturalist and field artist.

resources.title: Nature Journaling for a Wild Life
resources.author: Roseann Beggy Hanson
resources.rationale: Explore the enriching world of nature journaling in an eight-week, self-guided “workshop in a book” from explorer, naturalist, and artist Roseann Hanson.

resources.title: Redemption: The Church in Ancient Times
resources.author: Luke H. Davis
resources.rationale: Students will learn about Christians from this time period whose faith led many of them to martyrdom.  Beginning with Peter at Pentecost, the author tells about saints such as Ignatius, Cyprian and Athanasius who defended the truths of Christianity against heresy. Instead of just giving us facts about the different people, the author tells a story about a time in their life that helps the reader understand who they are and why their story is recorded in history. This volume is a great way for students to see how these important church fathers fit into the stream of history. These were the life and times they were living— when the persecution from the Roman Empire was prevalent.

resources.title: Renewal: The Church That Expands Outward
resources.author: Luke H. Davis
resources.rationale: Students will read the last half of the book this year as they continue to see how the church expanded globally through preaching, mission work, and social work in the name of Christ. The author tells the story of a pivotal event in the life of faithful Christians including William Wilberforce, Sojourner Truth, Hudson Taylor and more. Lesson plans will also include necessary background information, links, and other pertinent PDFs and articles.
resources.note: Use option one link to receive discount.

resources.title: The Gospel of Jesus: The Four Gospels in a Single Complete Narrative
resources.rationale: _The Gospel of Jesus_ weaves the entirety of the four gospels into a continuous, highly readable harmony that is placed in chronological order.
resources.note: Use option one link to receive discount.

resources.title: The Mysterious Voyage of Captain Kidd
resources.author: A.B.C. Whipple
resources.rationale: This is a biographical story about the late 17th-century Captain William Kidd, who was commissioned as a privateer, only to be accused of piracy. His adventures took him through ports and coasts along the Atlantic and Indian Oceans. By following Captain Kidd on his last mission voyage, students will discover the islands and places that were vital to merchants sailing in the West Indies and the Caribbean.

resources.title: Westward with Fremont: The Story of Solomon Carvalho
resources.author: Sophie Greenspan
resources.rationale: This book tells the true story of Solomon Nunes Carvalho, a Jewish artist and early photographer who joined explorer John C. Frémont on a dangerous expedition across the American West in the 1850s.  Students will come to see how maps were made from firsthand observation and why accurate geographic knowledge mattered for exploration and expansion. It is a fascinating read that follows the challenges of exploration—extreme weather, lack of supplies, illness, and difficult terrain.
resources.note: Use option one link to receive discount.
